sous_titres.text: Please, take care of him.
Turn off the lights.
Come on.
Careful.
I think your girlfriend wants sunglasses now.
No? Sunglasses for the beautiful woman.
Can you take care of Thanasis?
Please.
- Yes, of course. - I need to go to hospital.
Okay.
- Is he okay? - Yes.
He is healthy and all clean.
He's perfect.
Is there any way to contact your husband, Veronica?
Uh, he's working in Árgos. There's no telephone.
He's going to get a surprise.
Yes, he will.
I just need some more information for the, uh, Universal Health Service paperwork.
I can never remember it.
It's okay. I will take a copy of your residency card.
It's at home. I was at work when I went into labor.
Okay.
Here.
How was your trip?
Very long.
How have things been here?
Oh, uneventful.
Vera?
You only have to think about what's in front of you.
Vera, I have another son.
Thanasis Rotimi, look at your baby brother.
Look at your baby brother. Look.
Friends come and go, but family's forever.
You hear me?
- Huh? - You will never be alone.
What shall you call him?
Giannis. In Greek, it means "John," like the apostle.
And Ugo in Ibo means "a crown."
A crown that God has given, nobody can take away.
- Amen. - Amen.
- Amen. - Amen.
Sorry. You misspelled our last name.
- There's too many letters. - "Antetokounmpo."
Adetokunbo.
Antetokounmpo?
- Adetokunbo. - Excuse me, Ms. Pantazis?
Tell them I'll be a minute.
Are you sure we got it wrong? Our translators are usually very good.
We know our own name, Ms. Pantazis.
It's Adetokunbo. It's a very proud Yoruba name.
It means "the king from across the seas has returned."
Okay, I'll make a note of it.
Uh, did you bring your education history and proof of current employment?
Oh, yes.
Uh, so, this is a letter from the temp agency that gets us jobs.
This isn't enough.
You need to prove you're working legally, on payroll.
If you file a residency application like this,
it will be rejected on the basis you entered Greece without permission.
But we didn't choose to be illegal.
Our passports were illegally confiscated by the European police.
We couldn't go back because we had spent all of our savings.
We had to cross the border. You understand?
Now, our boys are Greek.
They need the rights that go with that.
Stay hidden while we work this out.
Excuse me.
Wait, Ms. Pantazis?
The longer we stay hidden, the more likely we get caught.
We just want to do the right thing.
You can file for asylum status.
Asylum? No, no, we are immigrants, not refugees.
Hmm?
There was no war where we came from.
If there was, we'd be refugees, but there wasn't.
Then find legal work. Stop using temp agencies.
Find a job that puts you on payroll and gets you a Social Security number.
That will qualify you for a work permit.
You can open a bank account.
We can take all that and present a strong residency application, hmm?
Ms. Pantazis,
no one will give us a payroll job without legal residency.
And no one will give us residency without a payroll job.
Okay…
Maybe sunglasses.
Watch your brother, eh?
You're not buying for you, Gianni.
I know, but how will I know people will like it
and want to buy it if I don't like it first?
You're absolutely right.
You have to believe in what you sell.
But how about you keep this one for you, huh?
We buy only what we can sell.
Sunglasses are good. Watches and...
Alex, Kostas, these rosary beads sell very well.
Hi, would you like a...
Excuse me?
Anyone? Anyone?
All right.
They won't stop for me.
They won't even look at me.
Let me teach you something.
The direct approach might not work for you.
You're not as handsome as me.
Wait. Wait. Wait. Wait. Trust your big brother.
You can't accept no.
No means maybe.
You're not selling this. You're selling you.
You have to find out what each person is interested in.
My brother sells the best sunglasses in Greece!
Huh?
For you, two euros. Just two euro.
Alex, Kostas, stay closer.
- Look at this. - Where did you get all of this?
- We sold all our stuff. - All of it?
- Yes. - Where's your hat?
- I sold that too. - Oh, my goodness.
Mama, police!
- Ugo, leave it. - We can't leave all this!
Rotimi, grab Kostas!
Mom!
Ugo! Rotimi!
I am winning!
Right. You click it now.
Mama, I can't see! Thano's going to score!
Sorry, Ugo.
He's gonna score anyway.
Over there!
- My boys! - Papa!
- Oh, yeah? - Papa!
Four to one! I will defeat you all.
I will defeat you all.
Oh, no! I grossly misjudged the situation.
Okay, let me go and say hello and goodbye to Mommy.
Oh! Ow.
- I need the ball! Now! - Mommy.
Oh, no.
You bought a soccer ball?
Eh, the old one burst.
I need to buy groceries.
We also have to pay rent.
Bamidele wants it today.
Ah. Don't worry. I didn't spend too much.
I promise.
Are you going to tell them they can't have it? Hmm?
- Pass it. - Pass it. Pass it! Pass it!
Pass it!
Pass it! Pass it!
- I'll shop smart. We'll be okay. - Pass to me! Pass to me!
Okay.
I have to go to work now. I'll be late.
Uh-uh.
- I'll miss you. - Pass to me. Pass it to me.
- Ow! - No ball in the house, Kostas.
I made plantain and yam for the boys.
Bye, my babies.
♪ Spread ♪
♪ Yeah, hey, yeah ♪
♪ Ofili ♪
- ♪ Hey, hey, hey ♪ - ♪ Tonight, music ♪
♪ Rewind, selector ♪
Thano, I'm open!
- Control it. - I'm open!
Now, Thanasis, pass to Giannis. Pass it. Pass the ball!
Goal!
See? Should've listened to your coach. Huh?
I could have scored!
Is it called Thanasis-ball? You have a whole team! Huh?
Ugo was in a better position to score.
Right?
If one man scores, the whole team scores. It doesn't have to be you.
Thank you. Eh?
Listen to your teammate.
One man scores, the whole team scores. Right?
Are you ready to go again?
- Are you sure? - Yeah.
- ♪ Yeah, hey, hey ♪ - ♪ Yo ♪
Come. Let's score.
Where's your team? You ready to score? Go over there.
- Let me score! - Score, score, score!
Goal!
♪ Brought up the mikes At the party, your place ♪
♪ Ah, ah, ah Yeah, hey, hey ♪
♪ Call the police when I enter the place ♪
♪ Pretty girls start to Wind up their waist ♪
♪ Brought up the mikes At the party, your place ♪
♪ Ah, ah Yeah, hey, hey ♪
♪ Call the police when I enter the place ♪
♪ Pretty girls start to Wind up their waist ♪
♪ Brought up the mikes At the party, your place ♪
♪ Ah, ah, ah Yeah, hey, hey ♪
♪ Call the police when I enter the place ♪
Alex! Kostas! It's time for a break.
There you go.
You want plantain? Hmm?
Foul! Foul!
Hey!
Do you hoop?
- Hoop? - Basketball.
Sure, we'll play.
- What about Papa? - He can see us.
Come on.
- Stop, stop, stop. - That's a travel.
- You can't do that. That's traveling. - It's what?
Gianni, you got to keep bouncing it, bro.
Keep bouncing it?
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
Hey, hey, hey.
The basket, man.
- Go put the ball in the basket. - Bro. Ugo.
Put the ball in the basket.
- Over here. - Shoot!
Hey, hey, hey! Pass it.
Tag.
Giannis. Thanasis.
Oya, second half now.
You should come down to the gym and play.
Youth club. It's free.
Where?
Filathlitikos.
Okay.
Welcome back to the top ten African basketball players.
And at number two from Cameroon and now living and playing in Greece,
Sofoklis Schortsanitis, known as Baby Shaq.
Whoa.
Man, he's like, "Is somebody here the Hulk of basketball?"
And at number one is the first pick of the 1984 NBA Draft.
Hakeem Olajuwon from Nigeria.
He was a soccer player and didn't start playing basketball till five years ago.
Look at the footwork from Olajuwon.
Fake, baseline and he scores.
Hakeem the Dream, what a move!
- Oya, boys. Time for bed. - If he was the only player on the court,
I could watch him play all night...
Forgive us our trespasses as we forgive those who trespass against us.
Lead us not into temptation but deliver us from evil. Amen.
Amen.
I love you.
And you.
And you.
And you.
Oya, go to bed.
- Good night. - Good night, Ma.
- Good night, Ma! - Good night, Ma.
I wanna play again.
You couldn't even bounce the ball.
Ooh!
And how would you know?
Every time I looked over there, you were stuffing your face with plantains.
- Ooh, true. - We could see you.
The ball kept rolling through your legs.
Well, you saw they didn't get the ball, did they?
- Yo. - It's called defense.
Filathlitikos is one hour, 20 minutes away.
We'll have to ride to get the bus, then change to the Metro.
I don't know. Papa won't like that.
And who's going to tell him?
Why are you looking at me?
Listen, go after school tomorrow. Get home before he's back from work.
Yeah, and we can get back home before he even knows what happened.
And I'll come to make sure you get there safely.
No, bro. Too little.
I'm growing.
Listen, since this is a top-secret mission,
me and Gianni have to scope it out first.
If it's safe, maybe we'll take you.
Maybe?
I just grew.
It'll change to "maybe not" if you don't go to sleep now.
- I grew more! - Sleep.
You too. Sleep.
Listen, Papa always talks about opportunity.
What if this is it?
The boy said it's a big club.
If we're good, maybe they pick us for their team.
Basketball players can make money.
We can help Mom and Dad.
You think you're that good already?
If it's to help our family, I will get that good.
MUNICIPAL GYM OF ZOGRAFOS
Gianni.
No wonder we're in last place.
We're terrible. Completely 100% terrible.
Who are these guys?
You know these guys?
They're tall.
What... What are you guys doing here?
Uh, we're... we're here to play basketball. At the, uh... the... the free youth camp.
Yes, we need it to be really, really free.
Do your parents know that you're here?
Uh… Our mom dropped us off.
She'll be back later. She wanted us to do the... the free...
The free youth club. Yeah, I heard that.
But we're gonna need her signature before you can play.
But she doesn't know we're here.
Gianni. Man.
- Where are you guys from? - Sepolia.
Wait here.
Why did you have to tell him we're from Sepolia?
What did you want me to say? Ireland?
All right, you can play for today, okay?
Go and line up over there.
Move your feet!

Giannis, listen.
Relax, man. Not too tight.
Relax. Relax.
♪ I won't win a Tony ♪
Just let it... let it fly. Like, like…
Break it.
♪ Check the time as I rhyme It's 1995 ♪
All right, never mind. Never mind.
No, no, I try again. I try...

I don't have any work. I'm sorry.
Well, if you ever need any work done, please call me.
Keep your head down, huh?
This neighborhood, it's not safe.
Where did you get that?
We went to play basketball after school.
What basketball?
Filathlitikos. They have a youth camp there. We...
You went across town without asking me or your mother?
- Papa… - To play basketball?
…Filathlitikos is one of the best teams in Greece.
The camp feeds the men's team. It's free. No questions asked.
Papa, the coach gave us this.
They said if you sign this, we can play every day after school.
What if they had reported you?
You disobeyed your father and took your younger brother with you.
- Papa, wait... - We did it together, Papa.
If we were in Nigeria, you both would've been beyond grounded…
- Papa… - …for a week.
Mama?
My friend, leave that basketball.
Let their punishment be a lesson, eh?
Promise me never to disobey your parents.
- We promise. - Good.
Can you imagine?
These boys are gonna kill me.
Why are you reacting so crazy? It's just a couple of hours after school.
When they are at school, I know where they are, okay?
They come home, they're with us.
We sell, we sell together.
Okay, this is only going to jeopardize that
and distract them from getting an education.
Shebi, we always said we wanted to protect their childhood.
With coaches we don't know giving us papers to sign, eh?
Now strangers watching them play?
Don't you remember when you used to play football?
We should let them play.
They need to have fun like normal children.
Vera, we cannot take this risk until we are all safe.
I'm trying to keep this family together.
I think about all of us too.
All of the time.
Vera, I'm sorry.
Vera…
I'm sorry.
Okay?
Let these boys be happy.
♪ Me press trigger Me no press people button ♪
Ooh.
Yeah.
♪ Ten are for you So forget the next dozen, fool ♪
♪ Come face me with somethin' ♪
♪ Like how mi have 22 Inna mi somethin" ♪
You have to concentrate on this, man.
I know!
♪ Anytime you're ready Punahussy start war ♪
♪ See who now fi Spread it out more 'pon the tar ♪
Gianni, stay back! Get back!

Bring it in, guys.
Team Red, grab a ball.
You're gonna do it from basket to basket, guys. Okay?
Team Gray, you follow them, and I'll time you.
- Eight seconds. - Eight?
Eight.
Basket to basket.
From defense to offense, under eight seconds. Okay?
You leave the opponent no time to react when they turn the ball over.

Good job. Bravo.

Relax. You'll get it.
Don't worry.
Veronica, you are late tonight.
I'm sorry. I was at work.
Hello, Francis.
Stop crying. We haven't even said "hello" back yet.
I can't help it. I miss you.
Don't use all the water, Ugo.
You always shower as if you're washing all of Greece.
I'll be quick.
- Hey! The water's gone. - Uh-uh.
Charles…
I thought they gave us an extension to the end of the month.
There's no water?
I'll call the water company.
Good! Good! Good!
Good! Good! Good! Good! Go, go, go, go!
Bravo, Thanasis!
Easy. Take it!
Bravo!
Thano!
He has put in a lot of effort, huh?
So what do you want for them?
I, uh… I want them to be the best they can be.
Thanasis could play in the A-league.
Teams are asking about him already.
The national team maybe…
National team?
Yeah, why not? Who knows where he'll end up?
And Giannis, uh…
He's getting better.
Takis, I know how this game is played.
Football, basketball, it's all the same.
Mmm.
If you want to go pro, you start young,
and you hope the gods are smiling down on you.
And they don't often smile.
And if they do, our family will be exposed.
Way to go, Gianni!
Feeling good today.
Hey, Thanos.
Look at them. Lookit.
Hey. Show them who we are.
Who wants this more, eh?
That's an advance for my signature.
Panarios, another C-League basketball club here in Athens,
they saw Thanasis play, spoke to Takis.
They want him to switch clubs and play for their men's team.
Is... Is Takis okay with this?
No, he wants me to stay. But he says it's our choice.
He knows we need the money.
I told him I'm taking it for us.
I'm so proud of you, Rotimi.
Huh?
Ugo, Ndubuisi, Emeka, your brother is leading by example.
When one person in the family scores, the whole family scores.
That's not how Papa says it.
When one person in the family scores, the whole family scores.
That's not how I sound.

♪ Even the sun don't get up Before me ♪

♪ Wanted, wanted ♪

♪ Money, oh ♪
♪ Go ♪

♪ Yeah, yeah, yeah ♪
♪ One day, one day ♪
♪ One day I'll be somebody ♪
Sorry, sir. This check's no good.
No, no, no, that can't be right.
Sir, this is an official check from a basketball club.
Please, try it again.
I did, sir. It's no good.
Yeah.
Mm-hmm.
Okay.
What happened?
Takis says the club found out about our status and withdrew the offer.
Rotimi, I'm sorry. I really am.
Tell Mom and Dad I'll be out in a minute.
I'm okay.
They can knock us down.
They can ignore us, insult us.
They can tell us we shouldn't be here and call us names.
But they can't stop us from getting back up.
National news now.
Entry into Greece remains the preferred path
for thousands of refugees escaping war, trying to find safety in the EU.
The Greek government is unable to curb the flow of these illegal immigrants.
It has led to a rise in anti-immigrant protests,
and violence directed towards them.
The government is now convening a special council to decide...
I was going to do that.
I can help you.
It's okay.
Why did you leave Francis behind?
He was too young for the journey.
It was one of the most difficult decisions of my life.
I'm still not sure if we did the right thing.
We wanted to go to Greece in search of a better life,
but we couldn't get our Greek visa in Nigeria.
So we had to find another way.
But it took many years.
By then, Francis had been born.
Your father and I spent many weeks discussing it.
We couldn't take Francis because we'd heard stories about the European police
treating even legal African travelers like they were criminals.
The journey would've been too dangerous.
So we made our choice…
to leave him where it was safe.
To bring him over…
as soon as we were settled in Greece.
We stick together.
But sometimes we need to sacrifice for the family.
Don't cry, Mama.
Everything will be okay one day.
I promise.
God makes no mistakes.
Give it your all.
And then let God do his work.
Charles Adetokunbo with the ball.
He fires.
Oh! It's a goal! It's a goal!
The mathematical Charles Adetokunbo just scored again.
Charles the man.
He did it.
He did it!
Thunder now to inbound. Up five.
Trying to work the ball inside. Nothing there.
The dish off the down screen to Durant.
And he hits it.
I got this for you boys, huh?
Since you wore out the pair you were sharing.
Thank you, Papa.
Ah.
Thank you.
You worked hard.
Hmm.
You've shown commitment.
You earned it.
Let's go break them in.
Let's go.
Uh-uh. In this rain?
Yes.
Four, three, two...
It's okay. We'll go again.
Chin up, huh?
That's why we fall. We fall to get back up.
It's okay. It's okay. Shake it off, huh? Come back.
It's not where you start. It's where you finish.
The company has put me on the payroll.
They're sending me checks every week.
And they're making Social Security contributions.
Which I can't claim until I'm legal, but, uh…
This qualifies for work permit, right?
You're a janitor?
Yes, I'm the janitor.
This isn't something we can build a residency application from.
You said "payroll job."
I meant a real job.
Ms. Pantazis, this is a real job.
Something that requires skill.
I thought it'd be obvious.
Skill?
I'm sorry, Ms. Pantazis. What exactly is it you do here?
You sit there and... and you...
Y-you give us advice, and nothing ever gets done.
The government is never going to give us residency, are they?
They are more concerned with keeping people out.
Keeping people out.
Ms. Pantazis, did you know that the Greek civilization has its roots in Africa?
Oh, yes.
Zeus and Shango, the Yoruba God of lightning and thunder…
they are the same.
We can't mess this up or you'll end up on the government's radar and deported.
Giannis, you're starting today. I need you to step up.
Pass, pass, pass!
Hey, move! Easy.
Come on, man.
Pressure. Pressure.
Hands, Gianni! Hands!
Hands!
Man, man.
Gianni.
Give it up, blackie. You suck.
Chairman.
- Papa. - Hmm?
Why do they hate us so much?
Ugo, racists are ignorant.
Afraid of anything and anybody different.
Son, this country has given us free education.
Hmm?
Jobs. Freedom.
The rest we'll sort out for ourselves.
Okay?
Did he make a miraculous shot? Yes.
Has he ever done it before? You bet he has.
Will he ever do it again? Time and time again.
- Now, we'll get the rest to you next week. - No.
Shebi work has been slow.
Or you been spending all your money on your boys' basketball.
I get plenty people who will want take this place from you, and they pay on time.
Is that you? Huh? Huh?
Okay. I know that face.
Don't say one word.
I can explain.
Okay. Stavros's cousin owns a garage.
Okay, it just need a new battery.
Brake pads, oil change, carburetor. Hmm?
I fixed it.
We can't afford to buy a new car. We need to pay this month's rent.
Vera, listen to me. We only have 500 euros.
Uh-huh.
But with a car, we can go out of town, huh?
Sell to tourists.
We'll be the only ones there, huh?
Why don't you take me for a ride?
Yeah.
Well, at least he can hit a jumper now.
All right.
We're gonna be good this year.
Good.
Thano!
All right! Go.
Watch out! Good!
Thano, bud, go!
Thano, keep your feet light.
All right! That's why we're working all week, man.
That's why we working.
Thanasis is a star.
Yeah. Good, good, good, good. Go. Go, go, go. Nice.
Hands! Hands! Hands! Thano. Pressure. Giannis, pressure!
Hands!
Block it!
Do it! Do it! Do it! Do it!
And look at Giannis.
- Yes! - Yeah!
Go!
Yes!
He has potential.
Pressure, Giannis, pressure!
Go, go, go, go.
He's leading the whole team.
Go, go. Come on. Two.
You guys were hitting the ball, now this happens. Okay?
Watch a little bit what's going on!
- Calm down. - Calm down? You calm down!
It's okay. It's okay.
Are you okay? Are you...
Stop, stop, stop, stop.
It's okay.
Easy.
Gianni. Go.
This is your chance.
Lead the team.

♪ A king is born I told you before ♪
♪ A king is born ♪
♪ Oh, yeah ♪

♪ Ain't no one like me I'm a legend, legend, legend ♪
♪ A king is born ♪
They are as good as anyone, Paco.
And still, they have room to grow.
No GM's gonna take it seriously.
These guys are never gonna be able to play legally in the A-League.
- They don't have papers. - You can get them papers.
Look. I said I'd come as a favor to Mr. Gianakos, and I did. Okay?
They're illegal immigrants, Haris.
Teams will get fined for signing them, and they'll get sent back
to wherever they came from the moment it's public.
They were born here.
You don't say.
Hi. I'm Haris Eleftheriou.
That's Haris with one R.
And Eleftheriou also one R.
We scout players from the lower leagues to the A-League.
Very few are good enough, but Giannis was incredible today.
He has really turned the team around.
Your friend didn't seem to think so.
- Paco? - Mmm.
He's not my friend.
H-He is one of the most powerful general scouts in the league.
He can influence anybody and get any A-League team to pick up a player.
But he is not my friend.
If you think he's good, why don't you call the teams yourself?
I'd have to rep them first.
Excuse us, Haris.
Boys!
Oya. Sorry, sorry, sorry.
Sports agent.
Hmm?
So, you want to be my agent?
If you'd have me.
Cool.
Thank you.
You won't regret this. I promise you.
I-I will break my back for you.
I'm going to start making calls right away.
Hey, um, h-how many clients do you have?
You're the first.
Those are selling out.
Oh, yes.
- How far? - It's not working.
Eh?
Let me see.
You could have called us.
Uh-uh.
- We live here. - Oh, yeah? Pay the rent.
This is back rent, this month's rent and next month's rent.
Bamidele, look at those boys. They should be in bed.
You pull this again…
I will not be as civil.
Next time, I will call the police.
They'll handle you. All of you.
My key.
